body.Industry: Application Performance Monitoring (APM), Error Tracking, Developer Tools, DevOps, Open Source Software
body.LatestValuation: $3 billion (as of May 2022, Series E round)
body.FundingRounds: Seed (2015, initial traction round); Series A; Series B; Series C; Series D: $60 million (February 2021, at $1 billion valuation); Series E: $90 million (May 4, 2022, at $3 billion valuation, co-led by BOND and Accel)
body.KeyInvestors: Accel, BOND Capital, New Enterprise Associates (NEA), K5 Global, Salesforce Ventures, Draper Associates, Slack (strategic), The Pay It Forward Company (16 total investors per PitchBook)
body.EmployeeCountEstimate: ~423–439 employees (as of 2025–2026, per Tracxn, PitchBook, and GetLatka); grew ~75% year-over-year as of 2022; employee count grew 8% last year
body.RevenueEstimate: ~$99–$100M ARR (2022 data cited at ~$99M ARR; GetLatka estimates ~$100M for 2024; revenue reportedly ~$123M in 2023 per some estimates — figures vary by source and are not officially confirmed)
body.GrowthMetrics: Net ARR expansion of 130%+ YoY (as of 2022 Series E announcement); processes 790+ billion events/month; mobile events grew 100%+ YoY; serves 4 million developers and 90,000+ organizations in 146 countries; revenue tripled in ~2 years (per CEO, 2022); ~$366K ARR per full-time employee (Sacra estimate); self-serve model accounts for ~70% of revenue; 30x revenue multiple at time of Series E
body.RecentDealsAndExits: Acquisitions: Emerge Tools (May 6, 2025 — mobile app size and performance tooling); Codecov (code coverage reporting); Specto (mobile app profiling, 2021); Bugsee (mobile crash reporting). Investment made: Earthly (Seed VC, October 2020). Became a unicorn in 2021 (Series D at $1B valuation).
body.ComparableCompanies: Datadog, New Relic, AppDynamics, Grafana Labs, Honeycomb, Rollbar, Raygun, Backtrace, Airbrake, Loggly, Rookout, Instabug, FullStory, Highlight.io
body.DataConfidenceNotes: Valuation ($3B) is confirmed from official press release (May 2022) and multiple sources. Total funding of $217M is confirmed from official sources. Revenue figures ($99M–$123M ARR range) are estimates from third-party data providers (Sacra, GetLatka, Latka) and are NOT officially confirmed by Sentry — treat with caution. Employee counts vary slightly across sources (423–439) and reflect 2025–2026 estimates. Founded year is sometimes listed as 2008, 2011, or 2012 depending on source (2011 incorporation, 2012 open-source launch are most commonly cited). Investor list of 16 total per PitchBook; some sources cite 10–12 investors. No post-2022 funding round has been publicly announced as of July 2026.
